title: The Titan Warning
tagline: The ground shook again. And this time it didn't stop for three days.
arc_note: TITANS STIRRING — Second background appearance, significantly more serious than EP010. The tremors last three days. Old structures crack. The Olympus Sea behaves strangely. Zeus sends a message to his father. Athena's WHAT LIVES BELOW scroll fills two more pages. The kids are beginning to understand the scale of what's coming.

characters.focus: Zeus and Athena — their private alliance around the Titan threat deepens
characters.new_element: Zeus receives a reply from his father — offscreen, but its effect on Zeus is visible. He comes back more settled. He tells Athena: 'He knows. He said it's not time yet, but it will be.' She adds this to the scroll.

scenes.title: Day One
scenes.narration: The second round of tremors started on a Tuesday and did not stop. Not constant — intervals of an hour, sometimes two. But they kept coming. By Wednesday the east face cracks from EP010 had lengthened. By Thursday the Olympus Sea was showing tidal behavior that didn't match any moon position. Athena opened her WHAT LIVES BELOW scroll. She had been waiting for this.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Three-day montage — Tuesday tremor, Wednesday crack lengthening on east face, Thursday Olympus Sea with wrong-direction tide. Athena's scroll growing: the WHAT LIVES BELOW section gaining entries each day. The group going about their days but watching the mountain. It's not stopping this time. 16:9.

scenes.title: Zeus Sends a Message
scenes.narration: On the second evening Zeus did something he'd never done before in the show: he wrote a letter. A real one, formal, addressed to his father. He used his best scroll and his cleanest handwriting and his official crown-stamp. He didn't show anyone what it said. He gave it to Hermes — personally checked the address twice — and watched Hermes disappear in a golden blur. Then he sat down on the Olympus steps and waited.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Zeus at his desk, writing carefully — this is different from his usual energy, deliberate. Using his official crown-stamp. Handing it to Hermes with visible 'check the address' instruction. Watching Hermes go. Then sitting on the steps outside, waiting. The sky darkening. Another small tremor. He doesn't flinch. 16:9.

scenes.title: What the Others Do
scenes.narration: While Zeus waited, the others prepared in their own ways. Hercules reinforced the base of the three most stressed columns — correctly this time, without being asked. Perseus checked on Medusa's valley from the ridge, making sure the canyon walls were stable. Achilles ran the perimeter of Olympus three times, checking every crack, reporting to Athena. Athena documented everything. Archie brought her an extra quill when hers ran dry.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Each character preparing — Hercules at columns being careful and correct, Perseus at the ridge checking toward the valley, Achilles running the perimeter and reporting, Athena filling pages. Archie dropping an extra quill in front of her without being asked. The group taking the threat seriously but working rather than panicking. 16:9.

scenes.title: His Father Answers
scenes.narration: The reply came on the third morning. Hermes delivered it — address-verified, directly to Zeus, not the nearest Z. Zeus read it alone. He sat with it for twenty minutes. Then he found Athena. He said: 'He knows. He said it's not time yet, but it will be. He said we'll know when it's time.' Athena wrote this down. Verbatim. She underlined: we'll know when it's time. She said: 'That's useful.' Zeus said: 'I thought so too.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Zeus reading the reply alone — expression shifting from tense to settled. Then with Athena. His words. Her writing them down, underlining. The two of them quietly aligned around something the others don't fully know yet. Archie watching the scroll as she writes. 16:9.

scenes.title: Day Four
scenes.narration: The tremors stopped on day four at dawn. All at once — not gradually, just stopped. The Olympus Sea returned to normal. The east face cracks stopped lengthening. The mountain was quiet again. Hercules said: 'Is it over?' Athena said: 'This part.' He considered this. 'Is the other part coming?' She said: 'Eventually.' He nodded. 'Okay.' He went back to reinforcing the columns. The best response to 'eventually' is preparation.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Dawn — everything still. The Olympus Sea flat. Hercules asking the question, Athena's answer, his quiet nod and return to column-work. The mountain peaceful but the WHAT LIVES BELOW scroll now substantially fuller. Preparation continuing even in the quiet. 16:9.

scenes.title: What We Know Now
scenes.narration: Athena's WHAT LIVES BELOW section now had seven pages. She reviewed them: the first tremors, the second tremors, Zeus's father's reply, Hercules's column work, Perseus's valley check, Achilles's perimeter data, the tidal anomaly. She added a summary line at the top: It is coming. We will be ready. We will know when. She showed it to Zeus. He read it. He said: 'Add one more line.' She said: 'What?' He said: 'We won't face it alone.' She added it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena's scroll open — seven pages of WHAT LIVES BELOW entries. She adds the summary. Zeus reads it, asks for one more line. She adds: WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE. The scroll, full and ready. Archie perched above it looking satisfied. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: The bravest thing isn't pretending the scary thing isn't coming. It's looking at it clearly, writing down what you know, reinforcing your columns, running your perimeter, checking on your friends — and deciding that when it comes, you'll be ready. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — the group on the Olympus steps, the mountain solid behind them, the Olympus Sea calm. Athena's scroll in her hands. Zeus's crown straight for once. All five together, prepared. The east face cracks visible in the mountain behind them — still there, but not lengthening. 16:9.

lesson: Preparation isn't pessimism. Looking clearly at what's coming and getting ready is exactly the right response.
titan_arc_note: EP015: Second stirring, three days. Zeus contacts his father, gets confirmation. Athena's scroll: seven pages. The team quietly prepared. Next Titan appearance: EP020 — Season Finale.
youtube_title: The Ground Won't STOP Shaking... 😰 | Little Olympus Episode 15
next_episode_preview: Episode 16 — Hercules's Big Day! He did something right. On purpose. Nobody can figure out how he planned it.
